desc: Align production query-specific CLI argument validation so singleton value flags are handled consistently across symbols, references, implementations, and packages instead of silently accepting duplicate flags or flag-shaped missing values.
done_when: Production command-level tests prove `symbols --index <fixture> --name <value>`, `references --index <fixture> --symbol <value>`, `implementations --index <fixture> --symbol <value>`, and `packages --index <fixture> [--prefix <value>]` still succeed for documented invocations; `symbols`, `references`, and `implementations` reject duplicate singleton flags and reject a singleton flag followed by another `--flag` token as a usage failure with empty stdout and status 2; `packages` keeps rejecting unknown, duplicate, and missing-value `--prefix` usage the same way; valid full SCIP symbols are preserved exactly and no partial/fuzzy/regex matching is introduced; `make test validate-distribution` passes.
scope: cmd/scip-search query-specific argument parsing helpers and production command tests for symbols, references, implementations, and packages. Out of scope: shared `--index` parsing, loader behavior, traversal/query result selection, JSON payload schemas, installer/distribution behavior, and accepting any new query flags.
